title: Pricing Strategy
use_when: Use when setting price, changing packaging, comparing pricing models, evaluating freemium versus paid, or designing pricing experiments.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Pricing Strategy when the request is mainly a different lifecycle artifact; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Pricing strategy brief with model, package logic, willingness-to-pay assumptions, and validation plan
framework_fit: Best when customer value, buyer willingness to pay, competitive pricing, packaging, and adoption risk need to be weighed together.
failure_modes: Produces Pricing strategy brief with model, package logic, willingness-to-pay assumptions, and validation plan without tying recommendations to the user's Strategize stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Pricing Strategy as generic Business Model advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Pricing Strategy when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $pricing-strategy to turn this pricing context into a decision-ready Pricing strategy brief with model, package logic, willingness-to-pay assumptions, and validation plan.
examples.expected_artifact: Pricing strategy brief with model, package logic, willingness-to-pay assumptions, and validation plan
